page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs via hyperthreading. It features 96 GB of dedicated RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core allocation. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring in the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Passmark CPU extended instructions. Memory performance is highly capable, with large-block uncached read and write bandwidths reaching 276.52 GB/sec and 118.36 GB/sec respectively, both placing in the top tier. It also exhibits strong throughput in database operations, static web serving, and medium-model LLM prompt processing. However, text generation for large 70B LLM models is a relative bottleneck, falling into the lower performance tier due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a resource-density perspective, the n4-highcpu-48 offers a cost-effective profile for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. By maintaining a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it minimizes overhead costs associated with unutilized memory. This configuration is highly suited for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, image processing, high-throughput web serving, and database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 96 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel processor with 24 physical cores. It includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. The instance does not feature bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high large-block memory bandwidth, and excellent throughput in database operations, ray tracing, and static web serving. Conversely, large-scale LLM text generation is a performance bottleneck due to the absence of dedicated GPU accelerators. This server is highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that require dense CPU resources without the overhead of excess memory, making it well-suited for software compilation, image processing, and high-throughput web serving. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio ensures resource density for parallel processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance without local storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support for matrix operations.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring advanced matrix extensions and strong multi-core performance for CPU-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 96 GB RAM powered by an Intel processor with 24 physical cores
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and large-block memory bandwidth benchmark results
No bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware
Optimized for multi-threaded compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving
